block.comment: The golden ratio
This is the angle, by which the branches are tilted. It is the angle at which five regular triangles meet.
A single branch with wedges that snaps together with other parts. Size is specified using 'length'. Clearance is the (tiny) gap between snowflakes.
The height of the wedge is where it meets the leaning branch. Other values possible.
The width of the wedge is measured at the base of the branch
Other values are possible for wedge thickness,
Angle between snowflakes.
Regular triangle whose circumcircle given radius.
The actual height of the spike. PHI times base length (other values possible)
The length of the base after tilting the spikes so that they meet in a pentagon.
extra height needed due to tilt so that the base can be made flat
extra length at the base due to the extra height
Elevation of the "ring". About 24% of the height (looks good, but other values possible)
ratio used as scale factor: the higher up the ring the smaller its width (since the spike tapers).
offset caused by clearance (30 deg angle to y-axis)
Length of the side (of the "ring") that is in contact with the spike
Length of one segment of the ring. Two such segments meet between each pair of spikes. Other values possible.
Height of ring. Other values are possible. This one results in a pentagram shaped ring.
Elevation of the small spike. About 38% of the height (looks good, but other values possible)
ratio used as scale factor: the higher up the ring the smaller its width (since the spike tapers).
length of the small spike's base
The position of the "hinges" (rotation axis)
For the big spike, the clearance also needs to be considered
This is the main spike of each branch
cut it to base_proj along x-axis (adding extra_h has made it too long)
cut everything below the base (adding extra_h has made it too high)
This is part of the "ring": two segments which meet at each spike.
Oversized cutoff, which sets height of ring
Creates a "spike" with given length (along x-axis) and height (along z-axis). Clearance specifies gap between the spikes (use zero for no gap).
This is the diamond-shaped cross-section of the spike with given length across. Clearance specifies the gap between spikes (zero for no gap).
offset caused by clearance (30 deg angle to y-axis)
